title: Edge Security, WAF, Headers, and Origin Containment · Inference Brief: Geo Privacy Boundary
summary: Predict unknown input, typed state, command, stream, promise, storage, cache, security, cost, and recovery outcomes before reviewing layered edge-security plan; use schema-controlled edge policies, layered authorization, observable false positives, private-error cache rules, and bounded degradation states. Planned artifact: Add and verify the geo privacy boundary increment in layered edge-security plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggi…
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, use geographic restrictions only with documented business, legal, accuracy, accessibility, VPN, and privacy limits.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, set HSTS, MIME sniffing, frame, referrer, content security, cross-origin, and content-disposition headers appropriate to file types and embedding.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Predict Geo Privacy Boundary · Theory Geo Privacy Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 9787 during a multi-tenant authorization audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to exhaustively evaluate application, WAF, origin, header, and private-error controls; bound bytes, time, attempts, promises, streams, concurrency, cache, and spend; then replace one typed fixture with unknown runtime input and prove rejection before AWS use. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Geo Privacy Boundary contract for the supplied data.
